final_answer:
Generated the question catalog from a frequency-tiered vocabulary of 16 words: classified 12 candidates smallest-first into the four-way distinction (grammatical-and-meaningful, grammatical open-slot, fragment, ungrammatical) and answered the first 6 grammatical-and-meaningful questions with the deterministic engine. The answered questions are a reviewable recall table — a repeated question is answered from the catalog instead of re-derived — and nothing changes solver behaviour without the human-gated learning ledger. Neural inference is not used; every candidate, class, and answer is a deterministic function of the lexicon.

task: Generate all possible questions from smallest to largest, drawing words from the frequency-tiered vocabulary, classify each candidate as grammatical or not and, among the grammatical ones, logically meaningful or not, then answer the meaningful ones and record the whole question catalog in Links Notation.
